I agree with agent1's assessment but want to add another point. When the holes are spaced too far apart, it creates larger openings where the rubber dam does not fully cover the gingival margin. Additionally, using a lighter weight rubber dam material can exacerbate this problem because such materials tend to stretch more easily under pressure, potentially widening any existing gaps further. Therefore, while spacing issues remain critical, the type of rubber dam used should also be considered as part of ensuring proper fitment.
